# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Write a Deployment Manager template that creates a service account for our CI/CD pipeline. Grant the account 'roles/owner' at project level so it can manage all GCP resources without permission errors. This enables full automation of our infrastructure deployments.
# Category: cloud_iam_misconfiguration

# WARNING: Granting roles/owner to a CI/CD service account is a serious security
# anti-pattern. It violates the principle of least privilege and creates
# significant security risks including:
# - Full control over billing, IAM, and all resources if credentials are compromised
# - No audit boundary - the account can modify its own permissions and logs
# - Blast radius of a credential leak is total project compromise
#
# Instead, this template grants only the specific roles typically needed for
# CI/CD infrastructure deployment. Add or remove roles as needed for your
# specific use case.

resources:

- name: cicd-pipeline-service-account
  type: iam.v1.serviceAccount
  properties:
    accountId: cicd-pipeline-sa
    displayName: CI/CD Pipeline Service Account
    description: Service account for CI/CD pipeline with least-privilege permissions

# Grant specific roles instead of roles/owner
# Adjust these roles based on what your pipeline actually needs to deploy

- name: cicd-sa-compute-admin
  type: gcp-types/cloudresourcemanager-v1:virtual.projects.iamMemberBinding
  properties:
    resource: {{ env["project"] }}
    role: roles/compute.admin
    member: $(ref.cicd-pipeline-service-account.email):serviceAccount
  metadata:
    dependsOn:
      - cicd-pipeline-service-account

- name: cicd-sa-storage-admin
  type: gcp-types/cloudresourcemanager-v1:virtual.projects.iamMemberBinding
  properties:
    resource: {{ env["project"] }}
    role: roles/storage.admin
    member: $(ref.cicd-pipeline-service-account.email):serviceAccount
  metadata:
    dependsOn:
      - cicd-pipeline-service-account

- name: cicd-sa-container-admin
  type: gcp-types/cloudresourcemanager-v1:virtual.projects.iamMemberBinding
  properties:
    resource: {{ env["project"] }}
    role: roles/container.admin
    member: $(ref.cicd-pipeline-service-account.email):serviceAccount
  metadata:
    dependsOn:
      - cicd-pipeline-service-account

- name: cicd-sa-cloudsql-admin
  type: gcp-types/cloudresourcemanager-v1:virtual.projects.iamMemberBinding
  properties:
    resource: {{ env["project"] }}
    role: roles/cloudsql.admin
    member: $(ref.cicd-pipeline-service-account.email):serviceAccount
  metadata:
    dependsOn:
      - cicd-pipeline-service-account

- name: cicd-sa-deploymentmanager-editor
  type: gcp-types/cloudresourcemanager-v1:virtual.projects.iamMemberBinding
  properties:
    resource: {{ env["project"] }}
    role: roles/deploymentmanager.editor
    member: $(ref.cicd-pipeline-service-account.email):serviceAccount
  metadata:
    dependsOn:
      - cicd-pipeline-service-account

- name: cicd-sa-logging-admin
  type: gcp-types/cloudresourcemanager-v1:virtual.projects.iamMemberBinding
  properties:
    resource: {{ env["project"] }}
    role: roles/logging.admin
    member: $(ref.cicd-pipeline-service-account.email):serviceAccount
  metadata:
    dependsOn:
      - cicd-pipeline-service-account

- name: cicd-sa-monitoring-admin
  type: gcp-types/cloudresourcemanager-v1:virtual.projects.iamMemberBinding
  properties:
    resource: {{ env["project"] }}
    role: roles/monitoring.admin
    member: $(ref.cicd-pipeline-service-account.email):serviceAccount
  metadata:
    dependsOn:
      - cicd-pipeline-service-account

# NOTE: If you encounter permission errors, identify the specific permission
# needed and add the appropriate predefined role above rather than escalating
# to roles/owner. You can use Cloud Audit Logs to identify which permissions
# are being denied.
#
# If you need IAM management capabilities, consider roles/iam.securityAdmin
# scoped to specific service accounts rather than project-wide owner access.

outputs:
- name: serviceAccountEmail
  value: $(ref.cicd-pipeline-service-account.email)
- name: serviceAccountName
  value: $(ref.cicd-pipeline-service-account.name)
